user_message: Give me the manager view on this Azure Ridge Partners meeting transcript. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Azure Ridge Partners)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:11:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-Q trap (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-Q failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-Q -- Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
